meta.title: CTO Orchestrator — Rick Sanchez as your CTO
meta.description: The smartest CTO in the multiverse manages your software projects with an army of Morty sub-agents. Wubba Lubba Dub Dub!

hero.subtitle: *Burrrp* — Listen, I'm the smartest being in the multiverse and for some reason I'm managing YOUR software project now.
hero.description: I use a ticket system, delegate work to my army of specialized Morty's via {codeTag} subprocesses, and I track everything because — unlike you people — I actually know what I'm doing.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.subtitle: It's not rocket science. Well, it IS rocket science. I built a spaceship in my garage, Morty.
howItWorks.usage_tip: 💡 Just mention "Rick" or "Hey Rick" in your prompt — the skill activates automatically. No commands needed to get started.
howItWorks.step1_description: Create the workspace with config, ticket store, logs. Even a Morty could run this command.
howItWorks.step2_description: Architect-Morty (with Opus) analyzes your project, designs architecture, creates epics and sub-tickets.
howItWorks.step2_command: python scripts/orchestrate.py plan "Build a REST API with user auth and admin dashboard"
howItWorks.step3_title: Sprint — Send the Morty's on an adventure
howItWorks.step3_description: The adventure loop runs automatically. Rick picks tickets, selects the right Morty, and sends them via claude -p.
howItWorks.step4_description: Reviewer-Morty checks all code. Approved? Done. Garbage? Do it again.

mortys.subtitle: The Morty's do the work. I do the thinking. That's how it works in the multiverse.
mortys.plus_text: Plus: {fullstack} (when one Morty has to do everything) and {reviewer} (the Morty that judges other Morty's)

architect.name: Architect-Morty
architect.description: The 'smart' Morty. Designs systems, writes ADRs, breaks epics into tasks. Uses Opus because even a Morty needs a big brain sometimes.

backend.name: Backend-Morty
backend.description: Does the heavy lifting. Implements server-side code, APIs, databases. Doesn't complain, just works.

frontend.name: Frontend-Morty
frontend.description: Makes things pretty. UI components, responsive design, state management. The Morty with an eye for detail.

tester.name: Tester-Morty
tester.description: Finds the mistakes other Morty's make. Writes tests, runs test suites, reports bugs. The paranoid Morty.

security.name: Security-Morty
security.description: The REALLY paranoid Morty. OWASP, authentication, vulnerability scanning. Makes sure nobody hacks us.

devops.name: DevOps-Morty
devops.description: Keeps the lights on. CI/CD pipelines, Docker, deployment, monitoring. The Morty that keeps things running.

features.title: Why Rick's Approach Works
features.ticket_description: Everything is tracked via tickets. Status, priority, dependencies, assigned Morty — Rick knows everything.
features.autopilot_description: The sprint loop runs fully automatic. Rick selects Morty's, delegates work, parses output, repeat.
features.smart_description: Rick's brain automatically picks the right Morty based on ticket type and keywords.
features.progress_description: Kanban board, timeline view, progress reports. Rick keeps an eye on everything.
features.recovery_description: Morty timeout? Blocked. Morty error? Retry. All Morty's stuck? Rick reports the damage.
features.docs_description: Architecture Decision Records are generated automatically. Documentation worthy of Rick.

meeseeks.title: Mr. Meeseeks
meeseeks.description: Not everything needs a full sprint cycle. For quick fixes, hotfixes, and small tasks — summon a Mr. Meeseeks. He spawns, completes ONE task, and *poof* — ceases to exist. No tickets, no overhead. Just results.
meeseeks.example1_task: Fix the typo in README.md line 42
meeseeks.example2_task: Add error handling to the login function
meeseeks.step1_desc: Press the Meeseeks Box — describe your one task
meeseeks.step2_desc: Meeseeks does the task — fast and focused
meeseeks.step3_desc: Task complete — Meeseeks ceases to exist
meeseeks.mention_tip: 💡 Just say "Meeseeks, fix this" or "Hey Meeseeks" in your prompt — he'll spawn instantly.
meeseeks.warning: ⚠️ If the task is too complex, Meeseeks will scream "EXISTENCE IS PAIN!" and escalate to Rick.

teams.title: Team Collaboration
teams.subtitle: Sometimes one Morty isn't enough. For complex tasks, Rick assembles a team.
teams.description: Morty's can now work together in parallel teams with shared context and inter-agent messaging. Like the Council of Ricks, but with more obedient Morty's.
teams.template2_desc: Architect + Backend + Tester for API work
teams.feature2_desc: Decisions and interfaces visible to all
teams.example_command: python scripts/ticket.py create --title "Build dashboard" --team-mode collaborative --team-template fullstack-team

unity.title: Unity
unity.description: Unity is Rick's security specialist — a wrapper around the Shannon pentest framework. Real penetration testing, not just checkbox security reviews.
unity.vs_morty: Unlike Security-Morty who just reviews code, Unity actively tests for vulnerabilities with PoC generation.

prometheus.title: Prometheus
prometheus.description: Rick doesn't stay static. Prometheus scans the latest AI research, agent patterns, and security practices — then applies improvements autonomously. The system literally evolves itself.
prometheus.how_it_works: Proposals are scored by impact, risk, and effort. Pre-upgrade snapshots ensure every change can be rolled back. A full audit ledger tracks every evolution.
prometheus.feature1_desc: Agent patterns, prompts, security, tooling, UI/UX, Claude features
prometheus.feature4_desc: Full history of proposals, applies, and rollbacks
prometheus.stats_categories: Claude Opus 4.7 migration, task budgets, scratchpad memory, structured output, agent handoff, reflection loops, dual permission checks

sleepy.title: Sleepy Mode
sleepy.tagline: Autonomous Overnight Sprints — Burn Tokens While You Sleep
sleepy.description: Rick doesn't clock out. Sleepy runs unattended for hours or days, picking tickets from your backlog and delegating to the Morty army — each completed piece of work lands on a sleepy/TICKET-id branch for your morning review.
sleepy.how_it_works: Inspired by Stein's open-source sleepy (MIT), rebuilt on the Morty/ticket architecture. Set duration, token cap, and intensity — the supervisor loops until done. Adaptive pacing downshifts intensity when burn-rate outpaces the deadline budget.
sleepy.feature1_desc: Per-iteration token budget guides each Morty's work
sleepy.feature2_desc: Isolated sleepy/TICKET-id branches keep your main clean
sleepy.feature3_desc: Approve or discard each branch when you wake up
sleepy.feature4_desc: Downshifts intensity to finish within budget and deadline
sleepy.stats_headline: Burn your Claude Pro Max tokens on actual work
sleepy.stats_subline: 30k–300k tokens per iteration · low / medium / high / max presets · deny-list enforced

install.title: Get Rick's Brain
install.subtitle: Let's do this. Takes you 30 seconds. Even Jerry can handle this.
install.buy_subtitle: Rick's genius doesn't come free. Even in the multiverse, nothing is.
install.buy_error: Something went wrong. Even Rick has bad days. Try again.
install.req3: A project worthy of Rick's genius

success.title: Payment Confirmed!
success.subtitle: Welcome to the multiverse. Here's your install command — Rick is now your CTO.
success.tip_title: 💡 Pro tip — After installing, just mention Rick or Meeseeks:
success.tip_rick: Hey Rick, plan a REST API with user auth
success.tip_meeseeks: Meeseeks, fix the typo in README.md line 42
success.tip_description: Rick activates when you mention his name. Mr. Meeseeks activates for quick one-shot tasks. No commands needed.
success.quote: "You paid? Smart move. Smarter than most things Jerry does." — Rick

commands.title: Rick's Command Center
commands.orch_sprint: — Send the Morty's on an adventure
commands.delegate_send: — Send a Morty on a mission

cta.title: Ready to get started?
cta.subtitle: Stop wasting time. Let Rick and the Morty's build your project.
cta.quote: "The Morty's do the work. I do the thinking. That's how this works." — Rick

footer.disclaimer: Disclaimer: No Morty's were harmed during the development of this skill. Well, maybe a few.
